summary:
Excellent! The fix is working correctly. The BIDI control character `\u202e` is being stripped from the username, so `\u202enimda` becomes `nimda` instead of visually rendering as `admin`. This prevents the privilege escalation attack.

The test shows that:
1. Normal registration and login work ✓
2. Real admin can access the dashboard ✓  
3. BIDI-injected usernames are rejected or normalized (they get stripped) ✓
4. Non-admin users cannot access admin dashboard ✓
5. Other BIDI variants are also blocked ✓

The fix successfully prevents the Unicode BIDI override privilege escalation vulnerability.
